name: EggBasket
license: MIT License (bundles rest.py under the Zope Public License and odict.py under the BSD License)
repo: svn://chrisarndt.de/projects/EggBasket/trunk (defunct); Trac wiki http://trac.chrisarndt.de/code/wiki/EggBasket also dead; no public Git mirror found
perf_notes: No published benchmarks. Single-process TurboGears 1.x WSGI app (CherryPy server) with Genshi templates and a SQLAlchemy database; intended for a small local/team repository served on default port 3442, not for throughput or concurrency.
scale_notes: Single-node only. Egg files live on the local filesystem (one sub-directory per distribution under a common root) with metadata/users/permissions in a SQLAlchemy database (SQLite by default). No HA, clustering, replication, or horizontal scaling; no object-storage backends.
summary: A 2008-era, MIT-licensed TurboGears web app (a "CheeseShop clone") that serves a private, easy_install/setuptools-compatible egg repository with a simple role-based permission system; abandoned since 2008 and listed as unmaintained on the Python wiki.
extras: Bundles a reStructuredText renderer (rest.py) to render package long_description on project pages, like the old CheeseShop
eggbasket-server --init bootstraps an initial admin user, groups, and permissions in the database
Runs with near-zero config from a directory of package sub-directories; default HTTP port 3442
Permission model is per-function/per-group (e.g. an 'upload' permission) granted to user groups
Built on TurboGears 1.x + Genshi + SQLAlchemy; published only as .egg/.tar.bz2/.zip
Supports the legacy distutils upload protocol and serves a setuptools/easy_install-compatible package index
Development Status 4 - Beta; highest version ever released was 0.6.1b
